pages.title: Research Methodology
pages.description: How the Raigulus Division Lore Archive separates evidence, canon, interpretation and uncertainty.

sections.heading: No source, no claim
paragraphs: Every factual lore statement is recorded as a claim and linked to one or more registered sources. A source must support the exact scope of the claim; a related page or search snippet is not enough.
Direct game captures and official publications are preferred. Community material can identify research leads, but it cannot be the only support for a published factual claim.

sections.heading: Fact, fiction and comparison
paragraphs: Real-world records, Division continuity and editorial comparison are labelled separately. Similarity is not treated as proof of influence, and contradictions remain visible rather than being resolved by guesswork.

sections.heading: Human publication gate
paragraphs: Automated research may reach source-reviewed status, but only a designated human editor can approve human-reviewed or published status. Review dates and unresolved notes remain attached to each record.

pages.title: Copyright and Quotation Policy
pages.description: The archive publishes original summaries and evidence locators rather than wholesale copyrighted scripts or media.

sections.heading: Public pages
paragraphs: Collectible pages should publish identifying metadata, original summaries, key lore points and precise locators. Short quotations may be used only when necessary and appropriate.

The public repository must not contain complete game scripts, wholesale collectible transcripts, subtitle dumps, extracted audio or video, or substantial book passages without a separate rights review.

sections.heading: Private research material
paragraphs: Full working transcripts may be used privately for checking names, speakers and claims. They must stay outside the public repository unless their publication has been explicitly approved.

pages.title: Corrections and Evidence Requests
pages.description: How to report an error, source conflict or missing piece of Division lore evidence.

sections.heading: What to include
paragraphs: Identify the page and exact claim, explain the correction, and provide the strongest available source with a timestamp, page, chapter, mission, collectible or game-build locator.
Conflicting evidence is recorded as disputed until it can be responsibly resolved. Reports are not treated as verified merely because they are plausible or widely repeated.
